scope: Defines generated model-input bundle paths and artifact roles before any bundle outputs are written.
notes: The bundle root follows existing FEMIC instance convention.
The bundle root is generated-output space. P4.1b does not create the directory or write outputs.
P4.1c must use AFLB as the stand-table universe and final THLB as a managed-share overlay.
NTHLB equals AFLB minus THLB and remains unmanaged/full-retention forest that still needs untreated VDYP growth curves.

canonical_aflb_stand_universe.role: canonical AFLB stand-table universe for P4.1c bundle generation
canonical_aflb_stand_universe.source: regenerated or rematerialized from the accepted Phase 2 AFLB checkpoint/partitions

readable_aflb_checkpoint.role: readable GeoPackage mirror for AFLB stand-universe GIS QA and maintainer inspection
readable_aflb_checkpoint.source: same regenerated/rematerialized AFLB state as the Feather checkpoint

final_thlb_overlay_checkpoint.role: final THLB overlay for managed share, unmanaged/NTHLB retention share, and final THLB area reconciliation
final_thlb_overlay_checkpoint.source: regenerated or rematerialized from the accepted Phase 2 THLB recipe/audit

readable_final_thlb_checkpoint.role: readable GeoPackage mirror for THLB overlay/fragments GIS QA and maintainer inspection
readable_final_thlb_checkpoint.source: same regenerated/rematerialized final THLB state as the Feather checkpoint

source_checkpoint_manifest.role: records source command, source audit, area metrics, checksums, and regeneration/rematerialization status

generated_tables.role: bundle provenance, run metadata, source paths, row counts, checksums, and accepted caveats
one row per AFLB stand or accepted fragment with stable keys, area, THLB/NTHLB share, IFM, ORIGIN, AU, curve, treatment, transition, cedar, harvest-system, and embedded-identity fields
canonical Patchworks-facing AU table; static AU identity and selected/remapped curve families
curve metadata table for natural/untreated and treated/managed curve IDs
age-by-curve yield points for Patchworks ForestModel export
AFLB stand-to-AU and selected curve-family remap audit table; every AFLB row must have an untreated curve or explicit fatal missing-curve rationale
stand-level natural/treated ORIGIN and curve-provenance assignment audit
accepted treatment IDs and eligibility gates for base and NICF-gated scenario hooks
accepted state-transition rows and source/destination state semantics
reporting and scenario group membership for TFL 6 base, WFP remainder, K3Z reference, expansion pools, cedar signals, harvest systems, and THLB/IFM classes
cedar-leading, Cw/Yc, cedar-present, old-cedar, unresolved utility-pole, reserve-context, and harvest-candidate signals
K3Z/NICF reference, outside-AOI expansion candidate/rejected/unreviewed, and WFP/TFL6 remainder identity fields
ground-based, cable, heli, and operability/slope proxy assignments and reporting classes

qa_tables.role: P4.1d summary of readability, row counts, required fields, and fatal/warn QA results
area checks across source input, THLB audit, final bundle, IFM, embedded identity, and reporting groups
missing AU, curve, treatment, transition, harvest-system, cedar, and embedded-identity mappings
natural/treated curve coverage and AU remap diagnostics
IFM, THLB, harvest-system, K3Z/NICF, expansion, and deferred treatment eligibility checks
cedar signal area/volume checks against P3.1b gross diagnostics and final THLB/IFM filtering
K3Z reference, current-AOI TFL6, WFP remainder, outside-AOI expansion candidate, rejected, and unreviewed pool checks

non_goals: assemble Patchworks runtime package in P4.1b
drop NTHLB forested rows from the model-input bundle
treat final THLB fragments as the complete Patchworks stand universe

stand_universe_contract.growth_curve_requirement: Every AFLB stand or accepted fragment needs an untreated VDYP curve, including NTHLB rows.

patchworks_semantics.THLB: managed treatment-eligible share subject to additional treatment gates
patchworks_semantics.NTHLB: unmanaged/full-retention share; compiles to UNMANAGEDAREA semantics downstream while preserving growth and reporting attributes
